summary: Frontend-only test for iter131 (Stripe modal compact + Vite HMR config). All validations passed — modal is ~1/3 smaller, typography scaled down, trust/PCI/help hidden in modal, functional preservation intact, full-page /payment/checkout retains original premium size, and Vite HMR connects over wss:443 (console shows '[vite] connecting...' followed by '[vite] connected.' with no ERR_UNSAFE_PORT).

modal_size_1440x900.status: PASS — width hit the 64vw cap (≈922px, below max-w-3xl=768 ceiling only because 64vw>768... actually 64vw=921.6 so max-w-3xl is NOT the winner at 1440w; the spec said 'sm:max-w-3xl sm:w-[64vw]' — w-[64vw] won here. Still ≤80vh and ~1/3 smaller than the prior sm:h-[92vh] sm:w-[96vw] sizing.

modal_functional.continue_to_stripe_button_clickable: Not clicked to avoid real Stripe redirect; button renders and its handler calls POST /api/checkout/session per source review

issues: Accessibility: Radix DialogContent emits console warning 'Missing `Description` or `aria-describedby={undefined}`' — title is sr-only but no DialogDescription is provided. Low priority, pre-existing.

critical_code_review_comments: StripeCheckoutPanel.jsx is ~340 lines and handles both modal and page variants via an isModal flag plus dozens of ternary className expressions. It's below the 700-line threshold but the density of inline conditionals is getting hard to read — consider extracting size-token objects (e.g., const T = isModal ? COMPACT_TOKENS : PAGE_TOKENS) to centralize the variant styling.
Modal DialogContent lacks an accessible description — add <DialogDescription className='sr-only'> to silence the Radix a11y warning.

Intent vs measured width nit: at 1440w viewport, `sm:w-[64vw]` (~922px) takes effect over `sm:max-w-3xl` (768px) because max-w caps but w-[64vw] sets the desired width and max-w-3xl would only clamp it if 64vw exceeded 768px — which it does (922>768). In practice the browser rendered 921.6px, meaning max-w-3xl is NOT being applied. If the intent was ~768px at desktop, drop `sm:w-[64vw]` or switch to `sm:w-full`. Either way it's still ~35% narrower than the prior 96vw, so acceptable.
seed_data_creation: None — 8 pending+unpaid orders already seeded for the customer.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Login flow: landing page → click 'Login to Your Account' → email/password form. Rate limiting can 403 on rapid re-runs; add 60s+ cooldown between back-to-back login scripts. Modal entry: /orders → Pay Now (8 unpaid orders available). Page entry: /payment/checkout?order_id=<id>. To grab an order id, click Pay Now and intercept the /api/orders/<uuid> network request (page.on('request')).
rca of the issue: No bug to RCA — both scope items verified working.
